de.uka.ilkd.key.core

Interface KeYDesktop

  • All Known Implementing Classes:
    DefaultKeYDesktop

    public interface KeYDesktop
    This is the interface to the desktop of the operating system. It should be used instead of Desktop, because the behavior is different in Eclipse.
    Author:
    Martin Hentschel
    See Also:
    Main.getKeyDesktop(), DefaultKeYDesktop

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      void browse(URI uri)
      Opens the given URI.
      void edit(File file)
      Open the given File for editing purpose.
      void open(File file)
      Open the given File for viewing purpose.
      boolean supportsBrowse()
      Checks if browsing URIs is supported.
      boolean supportsEdit()
      Checks if editing Files is supported.
      boolean supportsOpen()
      Checks if opening Files is supported.

    • Method Detail

      • supportsEdit

        boolean supportsEdit()
        Checks if editing Files is supported.
        Returns:
        true is supported, false is not supported.

      • edit

        void edit(File file)
   throws IOException
        Open the given File for editing purpose.
        Parameters:
        file - The File to open.
        Throws:
        IOException - Occurred Exception.

      • supportsOpen

        boolean supportsOpen()
        Checks if opening Files is supported.
        Returns:
        true is supported, false is not supported.

      • open

        void open(File file)
   throws IOException
        Open the given File for viewing purpose.
        Parameters:
        file - The File to open.
        Throws:
        IOException - Occurred Exception.

      • supportsBrowse

        boolean supportsBrowse()
        Checks if browsing URIs is supported.
        Returns:
        true is supported, false is not supported.

      • browse

        void browse(URI uri)
     throws IOException
        Opens the given URI.
        Parameters:
        uri - The URI to open.
        Throws:
        IOException - Occurred Exception.
